userStory: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
applicationContext: WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
entryConditions: Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at the exact URL:
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
Successful authentication returns the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
behaviorRules: The Login page must be opened through the `home_page` person/profile button for this workflow.
Password input must be masked by default.
Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully.
Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user.
Authentication failures, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, and incorrect credentials, must display only the generic `Failed` message.
Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed.
Failed authentication must maintain unauthenticated state.
Leading and trailing whitespace handling must follow implemented application behavior and be observationally validated.
Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to `home_page` without authentication.
Successful authentication must return the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
validationExpectations: The Login page opens successfully from `home_page`.
Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive.
Back and Home buttons are visible and functional.
Password input remains masked during entry.
Invalid credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Blank credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Partial credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
No dedicated required-field validation message is displayed.
Failed authentication does not transition the application into authenticated state.
Successful authentication using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated `home_page`.
Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login.
Whitespace-modified credential handling is validated according to implemented application behavior.
transitionExpectations: Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from `home_page` in `guest` state to the Login page.
Successful authentication transitions the application from the Login page to `home_page` in authenticated state.
Failed authentication keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise maintains unauthenticated state.
Invalid, blank, partial, and whitespace-modified credential failures must not transition the application into authenticated state.
Back button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Home button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Independent negative and return-navigation branches must begin from the canonical `home_page` `guest` entry context.
The unauthenticated entry state must be restored before executing each independent branch.
Transition from `home_page` to the Login page.
Validate Login page controls and behaviors.
Submit authentication using the Login button.
reuseGuidance: Reuse the existing Login page resource for:

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page controls are visible and enabled after navigation from home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible and enabled for interaction on the Login page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password field masks entered characters during credential entry
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field displays masked characters instead of the plain text password value while input is being entered.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful authentication using approved credentials returns user to authenticated home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to home_page in authenticated state, and authenticated-only UI and authenticated user identity indicators become visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username with valid password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify valid username with invalid password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no authenticated-only UI is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username and invalid password combination cannot authenticate
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication is rejected, the generic Failed message is displayed, the user is not redirected to authenticated home_page state, and no dedicated required-field validation message is shown.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank username and blank password submission displays generic Failed message only
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message is displayed, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank username with populated password displays generic Failed message only
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify populated username with blank password displays generic Failed message only
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation is shown, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify username with leading whitespace follows implemented authentication behavior observationally
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application processes the whitespace-modified credentials according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authentication state, navigation behavior, and any Failed message visibility are observable without assuming automatic trimming or rejection.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify username with trailing whitespace follows implemented authentication behavior observationally
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application processes the trailing-whitespace username according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authenticated or failed state is observable without assuming normalization.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password with leading whitespace follows implemented authentication behavior observationally
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application processes the whitespace-modified password according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authentication outcome and navigation state are observable without assuming trimming behavior.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password with trailing whitespace follows implemented authentication behavior observationally
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application processes the trailing-whitespace password according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authentication state and Failed message behavior are observable without assuming rejection or normalization.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify whitespace-only username and password submission remains unauthenticated
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page Back button returns guest user to home_page without authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state without exposing authenticated-only UI.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page Home button returns guest user to home_page without authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only functionality remains unavailable.

validations: The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible and enabled for interaction on the Login page.
The Password field displays masked characters instead of the plain text password value while input is being entered.
The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to home_page in authenticated state, and authenticated-only UI and authenticated user identity indicators become visible.

Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in unauthenticated state.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no authenticated-only UI is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.
Authentication is rejected, the generic Failed message is displayed, the user is not redirected to authenticated home_page state, and no dedicated required-field validation message is shown.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message is displayed, and the application remains unauthenticated.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation is shown, and the application remains unauthenticated.

The application processes the whitespace-modified credentials according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authentication state, navigation behavior, and any Failed message visibility are observable without assuming automatic trimming or rejection.
The application processes the trailing-whitespace username according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authenticated or failed state is observable without assuming normalization.

The application processes the whitespace-modified password according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authentication outcome and navigation state are observable without assuming trimming behavior.

The application processes the trailing-whitespace password according to implemented behavior, and the resulting authentication state and Failed message behavior are observable without assuming rejection or normalization.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state without exposing authenticated-only UI.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only functionality remains unavailable.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.

unresolvedGaps: The Login page model is missing the core Username field required for authentication workflows.
The Login page model is missing the Password field required for authentication and password masking validation.
The Login page model is missing the Login button required for credential submission and authentication validation.
The workflow requires explicit validation of the generic 'Failed' authentication message, but no failed authentication message element exists in the page model.
Customer signup button belongs to upstream home_page navigation and is not part of the Login page workflow ownership.
Clean survey button belongs to upstream home_page navigation and is not part of the Login page workflow ownership.
Site survey button belongs to upstream home_page navigation and is not part of the Login page workflow ownership.
The element name is implementation-centric and not business-readable.
This generic anchor element duplicates the Home navigation control already represented by the home button element.
Locator xpath=//a is dangerously generic and likely to match multiple links, creating brittle automation behavior.
Background content container is decorative/layout-only and provides no meaningful automation value.
The underlying shadow-native control is an anchor/link but is classified only as a button.
The workflow explicitly requires authenticated-state validation after successful login, but no authenticated user identity display or authenticated-only feature indicator is modeled.
